content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-high

## Summary

Accepted the amendments that fix concrete parsing/validation defects producing misleading errors or misclassifying bead scope. Rejected the two amendments that would remove an intentional escape hatch for explicit prompt overrides or would conflict with the current design choice to preserve arbitrary verbatim content inside `AGENTS / Repo Guidance`.

## Votes

- `fr-5-7eb75cd0`: Accept
  The reported behavior follows directly from the current state machine. When a later canonical heading appears after a missing section, `expected_index` is not advanced and `seen_positions` is not recorded for the found heading, so one omission turns into repeated `unexpected` errors plus false `missing` reports for headings that were actually present. Advancing past skipped sections and marking the found section preserves rejection while making diagnostics accurate and maintainable.
- `fr-5-d580dd6c`: Accept
  `split_bead_description_scope` currently treats any recognized section marker inside a fenced block as real structure, because it has no fence tracking at all. That means literal examples can be reinterpreted as `Non-goals` or `Acceptance Criteria`, which corrupts the rendered prompt. Skipping boundary detection while inside fences is the correct, robust fix and matches the validator’s existing approach.
- `fr-5-dcf32291`: Reject
  The current code appears to make canonical-contract validation conditional on the prompt actually declaring that contract. An explicit `--prompt-file` override without the marker is therefore treated as a generic custom prompt, and downstream stages intentionally gate their extra contract guidance the same way. That is an intentional escape hatch, not a correctness bug in the validator path. Tightening this would change supported override semantics rather than fix a defect.
- `fr-5-455d99e1`: Reject
  After `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance`, the remainder of the document is intentionally treated as verbatim guidance body, and tests explicitly allow canonical-looking headings there so embedded AGENTS content is not rewritten or rejected. While duplicates after the final section can look suspicious, the validator is only checking canonical outer shape, not prohibiting arbitrary markdown inside the AGENTS body. Rejecting all later canonical headings would break the current verbatim-preservation design.
- `fr-5-0405b9a2`: Accept
  This is a real scope-classification bug. Once `NonGoals` or `AcceptanceCriteria` mode is active, any markdown heading currently clears the mode, so subsection headings like `### Examples` cause subsequent bullets to fall back into must-do scope. That changes task semantics and can turn explicit non-goals into in-scope work. Restricting mode exits to actual canonical section boundaries is the safer behavior.
